If decomposed at Step 0, child beads cover each subcommand and this bead records the decomposition.","coverage":"non_scope: satisfied by this decomposition itself — children cover runs log/history (child 1), runs metrics (child 2), and tries (child 3); recording the decomposition is operator_required at the preclaim layer."},{"parent_ac":"6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.","coverage":"Child 'ddx runs namespace' AC5; child 'ddx runs metrics' AC4; child 'ddx tries namespace' AC4."},{"parent_ac":"7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","coverage":"Child 'ddx runs namespace' AC6; child 'ddx runs metrics' AC5; child 'ddx tries namespace' AC5."}],"child_ids":["ddx-b5f09750","ddx-331be3ca","ddx-74d09a3e"],"rationale":"Split along the two documented namespaces (FEAT-001 §21a 'ddx runs' and §21b 'ddx tries'), further dividing 'ddx runs' so the token/cost/usage consolidation (FEAT-001 §77-78 / FEAT-014) is its own independently testable subcommand. Child 1 establishes the 'ddx runs' root with log/history; child 2 attaches the metrics subcommand and depends on child 1; child 3 delivers the independent 'ddx tries' namespace. Each child reads only from existing executions/attachments/metrics stores, preserving the parent's no-new-persistence and no-removal-of-existing-surfaces non-scope constraints, and each carries its own Test* symbols plus the go test and lefthook gates."}
